subject: Databases
title: Naming Conventions
description: A micro-lesson that makes Naming Conventions usable.

hook.text: One table called Users, another tbl_order_data, another customerInfo. Inconsistent names cause chaos.

buildup.text: Pick a convention and stick with it: snake_case for columns, plural for tables, no prefixes.

discovery.text: user_id is clearer than uid. created_at beats cDate. Descriptive names save future developers hours.

twist.text: Renaming columns in production breaks existing queries. Get naming right early to avoid pain.

climax.text: Document the conventions in a style guide. New team members should know the rules from day one.

punchline.text: Consistent names prevent confusion. Choose a convention early.

quiz.question: Why is it important to establish naming conventions early in a project?
options: Renaming columns later breaks existing queries and causes widespread code changes
The database engine requires a specific naming format
Names have no impact on database functionality
